title: lathyrus niger f. longipes
intro: Lathyrus niger f. longipes, also known as the dark pea or vetchling, is a perennial legume plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its seeds, which contain a compound called lathyrine, a neurotoxin with potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The seeds of Lathyrus niger f. longipes have been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, although their consumption should be done under medical supervision. In herbal medicine, the seeds are often prepared into decoctions or infusions, and also used in traditional remedies for various ailments, but its preparation should be done with caution and under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
